description_long: This is a vintage gramophone-style record player with a large, flared metal horn that is weathered with green and rust hues, radiating from a dark central opening. The horn is attached to a greenish, ornate wooden base with carved details and a stepped pedestal design. On top of the base sits a circular black turntable platter with a visible vinyl record, and a metallic tonearm extending over it. The tonearm is dark and slightly corroded, with a jointed structure and a greenish section near the horn. A winding handle is present on the side of the base, and the overall finish is aged, with visible patina and wear. The object exudes an antique, decorative charm, suitable for display in urban vintage shops, parks, or themed public spaces.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large, flared metal horn, ornate wooden base, and visible turntable platter and tonearm. The finish is aged with green, brown, and metallic tones.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, flared opening of the horn with green and orange radial patterns and the ornate base beneath.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the horn, the tonearm extending over the platter, and the winding handle on the base.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the narrow end of the horn, the tonearm's attachment, and the full top surface of the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the horn's profile, the tonearm, and the detailed carvings on the base.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and black, occupying a central position on the wooden base. It is slightly raised, with a visible vinyl record on top, and appears to be made of metal or hard plastic. The platter has a matte finish and no visible grooves or patterns beyond the record itself.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and dark in color with a greenish section near the horn. It is jointed, extends over the platter, and appears to be resting in a playback position. The finish is aged and slightly corroded.
